meta.title: 丝绸之路知识图谱
meta.description: 一个关于丝绸之路的结构化开放数据集，涵盖城市、人物、物产、事件四大类实体及其关联关系。
meta.description_en: An open structured dataset about the Silk Road, covering cities, figures, goods, and events and their interconnections.

cities.name: 长安
cities.description: 丝绸之路的东方起点，汉唐帝国的都城。从这里出发的商队穿越河西走廊，将丝绸、瓷器、漆器运往西域。
cities.description_en: The eastern terminus of the Silk Road and capital of the Han and Tang empires. Caravans departed from here, crossing the Hexi Corridor with silk, porcelain, and lacquerware bound for the Western Regions.

cities.name: 洛阳
cities.description: 东汉时期的丝路起点，也是佛教传入中国的重要节点。白马寺是中国第一座佛教寺院。
cities.description_en: The Silk Road's starting point during the Eastern Han dynasty, and a key node for Buddhism's entry into China. The White Horse Temple is China's first Buddhist temple.

cities.name: 敦煌
cities.description: 丝路上的璀璨明珠，莫高窟所在地。出敦煌便进入西域，是东西方文明交汇的十字路口。
cities.description_en: A brilliant jewel on the Silk Road and home to the Mogao Caves. Beyond Dunhuang lay the Western Regions — it was the crossroads of Eastern and Western civilizations.

cities.name: 喀什
cities.description: 塔里木盆地西缘的贸易枢纽，天山南路和天山北路的交汇点。至今保留着浓郁的西域风情。
cities.description_en: A trading hub on the western edge of the Tarim Basin where the northern and southern routes around the Taklamakan Desert converged. It retains a strong Central Asian character to this day.

cities.name: 撒马尔罕
cities.description: 中亚最古老的城市之一，粟特人的商业中心。帖木儿帝国时期达到鼎盛，雷吉斯坦广场见证了丝路最后的辉煌。
cities.description_en: One of Central Asia's oldest cities and a commercial center of the Sogdians. It reached its zenith under the Timurid Empire — the Registan Square bears witness to the Silk Road's last golden age.

cities.name: 巴格达
cities.description: 阿拔斯王朝首都，智慧宫所在地。中国的造纸术通过丝路传到这里，再由阿拉伯人传往欧洲。
cities.description_en: Capital of the Abbasid Caliphate and home to the House of Wisdom. Chinese papermaking technology reached Baghdad via the Silk Road and was later transmitted to Europe by the Arabs.

cities.name: 君士坦丁堡
cities.description: 丝路西端的终点，连接亚洲与欧洲的黄金之城。中国的丝绸由此进入欧洲贵族的生活。
cities.description_en: The western terminus of the Silk Road — a golden city bridging Asia and Europe. Chinese silk entered the lives of European nobility through this gateway.

cities.name: 吐鲁番
cities.description: 火焰山下的绿洲城市，高昌故城所在地。是佛教从印度经西域传入中原的中转站。
cities.description_en: An oasis city beneath the Flaming Mountains, site of the ancient city of Gaochang. A relay station for Buddhism's transmission from India through the Western Regions into China.

cities.name: 楼兰
cities.era: 公元前2世纪 — 公元4世纪
cities.description: 罗布泊畔的神秘古城，曾是西域三十六国之一。楼兰是丝路南道的必经之地，却在公元4世纪突然消失，成为千古之谜。1900年斯文·赫定重新发现了楼兰遗址。
cities.description_en: A mysterious ancient city by the shores of the now-vanished Lop Nur lake, once one of the 36 kingdoms of the Western Regions. Loulan was a mandatory stop on the southern Silk Road but mysteriously disappeared in the 4th century CE. Sven Hedin rediscovered its ruins in 1900.

cities.name: 和田
cities.description: 塔克拉玛干沙漠南缘的古老王国，以和田玉闻名世界。丝路南道的佛教中心之一，玄奘归途曾在此停留，赞叹当地佛教之盛。
cities.description_en: An ancient kingdom on the southern edge of the Taklamakan Desert, world-famous for its jade. A major Buddhist center on the southern Silk Road — Xuanzang stopped here on his return journey and marveled at the flourishing Buddhist culture.

cities.name: 木鹿
cities.era: 公元前6世纪 — 公元13世纪
cities.role_en: Great Oasis City of Central Asia
cities.description: 曾被称为'世界之母'的中亚大城，位于呼罗珊大道上。一度是伊斯兰世界最大的城市之一，拥有宏伟的图书馆和天文台。1221年毁于蒙古西征。
cities.description_en: Once called the 'Mother of the World,' this great Central Asian city sat on the Khorasan Highway. It was one of the largest cities of the Islamic world, with magnificent libraries and observatories. It was destroyed by the Mongol conquest in 1221.

cities.name: 布哈拉
cities.description: 与撒马尔罕齐名的粟特重镇，中亚伊斯兰学术中心。布哈拉的商人网络遍布丝路，是东西方贸易的关键节点。古城至今保存完好。
cities.description_en: A Sogdian city rivaling Samarkand and a center of Islamic learning in Central Asia. Bukhara's merchant networks spanned the entire Silk Road, making it a critical node in East-West trade. Its old city is remarkably well-preserved.

cities.name: 赫拉特
cities.description: 呼罗珊地区的文化名城，帖木儿帝国时期的艺术中心。连接伊朗高原与中亚的咽喉，丝路南北两条支线的交汇点。
cities.description_en: A cultured city of the Khorasan region and an artistic center during the Timurid Renaissance. It commands the pass between the Iranian plateau and Central Asia, where two branches of the Silk Road converge.

cities.name: 泉州
cities.description: 宋元时期世界最大的港口之一，海上丝绸之路的起点。马可·波罗和伊本·白图泰都曾到访，称其为'刺桐城'。汇聚了来自波斯、阿拉伯、印度的商人。
cities.description_en: One of the world's largest ports during the Song and Yuan dynasties and the starting point of the Maritime Silk Road. Marco Polo and Ibn Battuta both visited, calling it 'Zayton.' Merchants from Persia, Arabia, and India converged here.

cities.name: 元大都
cities.description: 元代称为大都（汗八里），是马可·波罗的目的地。蒙古帝国的东方中心，汇聚了来自丝路各地的使节、商人、工匠和传教士，是当时世界上最繁华的都会。
cities.description_en: Known as Dadu or Khanbaliq in the Yuan dynasty, this was Marco Polo's destination. The eastern center of the Mongol Empire, it gathered envoys, merchants, artisans, and missionaries from across the Silk Road — one of the most splendid capitals of the medieval world.

cities.name: 尼萨（安息）
cities.era: 公元前3世纪 — 公元3世纪
cities.role: 波斯与东方之间的桥梁
cities.role_en: Bridge between Persia and the East
cities.description: 帕提亚帝国（安息）的首都之一。安息几乎垄断了丝路中段的贸易，也是唯一阻挡了罗马帝国东扩的东方大国。汉武帝遣使安息，双方建立了最早的丝路外交关系。
cities.description_en: One of the capitals of the Parthian Empire, which largely monopolized the middle segment of Silk Road trade. Parthia was the only Eastern power that blocked Rome's eastward expansion. Emperor Wu of Han sent envoys to Parthia, establishing the earliest diplomatic relations along the Silk Road.

cities.name: 塔克西拉
cities.era: 公元前6世纪 — 公元5世纪
cities.description: 印度次大陆的学问之城，犍陀罗文明的荣光所在。希腊化艺术与佛教信仰在此融合，产生了举世闻名的犍陀罗佛像艺术。玄奘和法显都曾到此求学瞻仰。
cities.description_en: A city of learning on the Indian subcontinent and the glory of Gandharan civilization. Here, Hellenistic art fused with Buddhist faith, producing the world-renowned Gandharan Buddha sculpture. Both Xuanzang and Faxian visited as pilgrims and students.

cities.name: 碎叶
cities.role_en: One of Four Garrisons of Anxi
cities.description: 唐代安西四镇中最西端的一个，是李白的出生地。碎叶城的设立标志着唐朝势力深入中亚草原，此地也是突厥文化和汉文化交汇的前哨。
cities.description_en: The westernmost of the Tang dynasty's Four Garrisons of Anxi and the birthplace of the poet Li Bai. Suyab's establishment marked the Tang empire's reach into the Central Asian steppe, and it was a frontier outpost where Turkic and Chinese cultures met.

cities.name: 赖伊
cities.description: 位于伊朗高原的古老城市，丝路上连接中亚与美索不达米亚的重要驿站。赖伊的巴扎汇集了来自东西方的商品，也是知识传播的中转地。蒙古西征期间遭到严重破坏。
cities.description_en: An ancient city on the Iranian plateau, a vital staging post connecting Central Asia with Mesopotamia. Rayy's bazaars gathered goods from East and West alike, and the city also served as a relay for knowledge transmission. It was severely damaged during the Mongol conquests.

cities.name: 广州
cities.role_en: Gateway to the South China Sea
cities.description: 中国最古老的海上贸易港口之一，唐代“广州通海夷道”的起点。阿拉伯、波斯、印度商人云集，市舶司最早在此设立。海上丝路的第一站。
cities.description_en: One of China's oldest maritime trading ports and the starting point of the Tang dynasty 'Maritime Passage to Foreign Lands.' Arab, Persian, and Indian merchants gathered here — the first stop on the Maritime Silk Road.

cities.name: 马六甲
cities.description: 马六甲海峡的贸易枢纽，郑和七次下西洋五次在此驻泊。控制着印度洋与太平洋之间的咽喉要道，谁掌握了马六甲，谁就掌握了东西方贸易。
cities.description_en: The trading hub of the Malacca Strait — Zheng He anchored here on five of his seven voyages. Controlling the narrow passage between the Indian and Pacific Oceans, whoever held Malacca held the key to East-West trade.

cities.name: 卡利卡特
cities.description: 印度马拉巴尔海岸的贸易中心，被称为“香料之城”。郑和的船队多次抵达这里，比达·伽马早了近一个世纪。胡椒、豆蔻、肉桂在此集散，运往中国和中东。
cities.description_en: The trading center of India's Malabar Coast, known as the 'City of Spices.' Zheng He's fleet arrived here nearly a century before Vasco da Gama. Pepper, cardamom, and cinnamon were collected here and shipped to China and the Middle East.

cities.name: 科伦坡
cities.description: 古称“狮子国”或“锡兰”，以宝石和珍珠闻名。法显归国时在此停留两年，郑和船队在此立碑。印度洋航线的必经补给站。
cities.description_en: Known to the ancients as the 'Lion Kingdom' or 'Ceylon,' famed for its gems and pearls. Faxian stayed here for two years on his return journey, and Zheng He erected a stele here. An essential resupply point on the Indian Ocean route.

cities.name: 霍尔木兹
cities.description: 波斯湾入口处的贸易岛城，马可·波罗和伊本·白图泰都曾到访。郑和船队第四次至第七次下西洋均在此停泊。中东香料、珍珠、骏马由此运往东方。
cities.description_en: An island trading city at the mouth of the Persian Gulf, visited by both Marco Polo and Ibn Battuta. Zheng He's fleet docked here on voyages four through seven. Middle Eastern spices, pearls, and horses were shipped east from here.

cities.name: 亚丁
cities.description: 红海与印度洋交汇处的天然良港，乳香和没药的主要出口港。从非洲运来的象牙、黄金、奴隶在此集散，中国瓷器、丝绸由此进入阿拉伯世界。
cities.description_en: A natural deep-water harbor where the Red Sea meets the Indian Ocean, and the main export hub for frankincense and myrrh. Ivory, gold, and slaves from Africa were transshipped here, while Chinese porcelain and silk entered the Arab world through this port.

cities.name: 亚历山大
cities.description: 海上丝绸之路的西端终点。中国货物经印度洋、红海抵达此处，再转运至威尼斯、热那亚和欧洲各地。亚历山大图书馆曾是古代世界最伟大的知识中心。
cities.description_en: The western terminus of the Maritime Silk Road. Chinese goods arrived here via the Indian Ocean and Red Sea, then were transshipped to Venice, Genoa, and across Europe. The Library of Alexandria was once the greatest repository of knowledge in the ancient world.

cities.name: 蒙巴萨
cities.description: 海上丝路在非洲的最远到达点之一。郑和船队曾抵达东非海岸，带回长颈鹿（被中国人误认为麒麟）。中国瓷器碎片至今在蒙巴萨海滩上被发现。
cities.description_en: One of the farthest points reached by the Maritime Silk Road in Africa. Zheng He's fleet reached the East African coast and brought back a giraffe (mistaken by the Chinese for the mythical qilin). Chinese porcelain shards are still found on Mombasa's beaches to this day.

cities.name: 巨港（三佛齐）
cities.description: 三佛齐（室利佛逝）帝国的都城，7至13世纪控制马六甲海峡的超级海上强权。义净在此学习梵文，前往印度取经前在此停留六个月。
cities.description_en: Capital of the Srivijaya empire, a maritime superpower that controlled the Malacca Strait from the 7th to 13th centuries. The Chinese monk Yijing studied Sanskrit here, staying for six months before continuing to India.

figures.name: 张骞
figures.description: 汉武帝派遣出使西域的使节，两次西行，凿空西域，被誉为'丝绸之路的开辟者'。带回了葡萄、苜蓿、胡麻等西域物产的信息。
figures.description_en: An envoy sent by Emperor Wu of Han to the Western Regions. His two expeditions 'opened up' the Western Regions, earning him the title 'Pioneer of the Silk Road.' He brought back knowledge of grapes, alfalfa, and sesame.

figures.name: 玄奘
figures.description: 唐代高僧，沿丝路西行至印度取经，历时17年，行程五万里。归国后翻译佛经75部，著有《大唐西域记》，是研究丝路历史的珍贵文献。
figures.description_en: A Tang dynasty monk who traveled west along the Silk Road to India in search of Buddhist scriptures. His 17-year, 50,000-li journey resulted in the translation of 75 Buddhist texts and the 'Great Tang Records on the Western Regions.'

figures.name: 马可·波罗
figures.description: 威尼斯商人，沿丝路到达元大都（北京），仕元17年。《马可·波罗游记》激发了欧洲对东方的向往，间接推动了地理大发现。
figures.description_en: A Venetian merchant who reached the Yuan capital of Dadu (Beijing) via the Silk Road and served the Mongol court for 17 years. His 'Travels' ignited European fascination with the East and indirectly spurred the Age of Discovery.

figures.name: 甘英
figures.description: 班超派遣的使节，出使大秦（罗马帝国），抵达条支（今叙利亚一带）的海边。虽因安息商人的劝阻未能渡海，但他是史载最接近罗马的汉朝使者。
figures.description_en: An envoy sent by Ban Chao to the Roman Empire (Daqin). He reached the coast of Tiaozhi (modern Syria) but was dissuaded from crossing the sea by Parthian merchants. He was the Han envoy who came closest to Rome.

figures.name: 班超
figures.description: 投笔从戎的传奇将领，东汉西域都护。在西域经营三十余年，使五十余国归附汉朝。曾派遣甘英出使大秦（罗马），将汉朝的影响力推至中亚腹地。
figures.description_en: A legendary general who 'threw aside the brush to join the army.' As Protector General of the Western Regions for over 30 years, he brought more than 50 kingdoms under Han suzerainty. He dispatched Gan Ying to Rome (Daqin), extending Han influence deep into Central Asia.

figures.name: 法显
figures.description: 中国第一位从陆路西行、海路归国的取经僧侣。399年以六十高龄从长安出发，经敦煌、于阗、翻越葱岭到达印度，归途经海路至斯里兰卡、爪哇，414年回到中国。著《佛国记》，是最早记录中亚和印度地理的汉文文献。
figures.description_en: The first Chinese monk to travel to India by land and return by sea. Setting out at age 60 in 399 CE, he traveled via Dunhuang and Khotan, crossed the Pamirs to India, and returned via Sri Lanka and Java — arriving back in China in 414. His 'Record of Buddhist Kingdoms' is the earliest Chinese geographical account of Central Asia and India.

figures.name: 鸠摩罗什
figures.description: 出生于龟兹（今库车）的混血高僧，父亲是印度人，母亲是龟兹公主。被带到长安后主持翻译佛经74部384卷，包括《金刚经》《法华经》《维摩诘经》。其翻译精准优美，影响中国佛教两千年。
figures.description_en: A monk of mixed Indian-Kuchean heritage born in Kucha. Brought to Chang'an, he led the translation of 74 Buddhist texts (384 volumes), including the Diamond Sutra, Lotus Sutra, and Vimalakirti Sutra. His translations are renowned for their precision and elegance, shaping Chinese Buddhism for two millennia.

figures.name: 伊本·白图泰
figures.description: 摩洛哥旅行家，行程十二万公里，远超马可·波罗。1346年经海路抵达泉州，游历广州、杭州，最终到达元大都。他称杭州是'世界上最美丽的城市'，其《游记》是研究14世纪欧亚非的珍贵史料。
figures.description_en: A Moroccan traveler who journeyed 120,000 km — far exceeding Marco Polo. In 1346 he arrived at Quanzhou by sea, visited Guangzhou and Hangzhou, and reached Khanbaliq (Beijing). He called Hangzhou 'the most beautiful city on earth.' His 'Rihla' (Travels) is an invaluable record of 14th-century Eurasia and Africa.

figures.name: 拉班·扫马
figures.type: 景教僧侣 / 外交使节
figures.description: 生于元大都的畏兀儿景教徒，从北京出发西行朝圣。在伊儿汗国被任命为出使欧洲的大使，会见了拜占庭皇帝、法兰西国王和英格兰国王。他是史上唯一一位从中国出发到达西欧的中世纪旅行家。
figures.description_en: A Uyghur Nestorian Christian born in Khanbaliq (Beijing) who traveled west on pilgrimage. The Ilkhanate appointed him ambassador to Europe — he met the Byzantine Emperor, the King of France, and the King of England. He is the only known medieval traveler to have journeyed from China to Western Europe.

figures.name: 李白
figures.description: 盛唐最伟大的诗人之一，出生于碎叶城（今吉尔吉斯斯坦境内），其出生地本身就是丝路文化交流的见证。他的诗'胡姬貌如花，当垆笑春风'描绘了长安城中西域女子的风情，展现了丝路带来的文化交融。
figures.description_en: One of the greatest poets of the High Tang, born in Suyab (modern Kyrgyzstan) — his birthplace itself is testimony to Silk Road cultural exchange. His verse depicts Central Asian women in Chang'an, capturing the cosmopolitan world the Silk Road created.

figures.name: 汉武帝刘彻
figures.description: 在位54年，派遣张骞出使西域，北伐匈奴，开通河西走廊。他主导的一系列军事和外交行动，实质性地打通了丝绸之路，使中国第一次与中亚、西亚建立了直接联系。
figures.description_en: Ruling for 54 years, he dispatched Zhang Qian to the Western Regions, campaigned against the Xiongnu, and opened the Hexi Corridor. His military and diplomatic initiatives substantially opened the Silk Road, establishing China's first direct contact with Central and Western Asia.

figures.name: 王昭君
figures.description: 中国古代四大美女之一，出塞和亲匈奴呼韩邪单于。她的和亲换来了汉匈五十余年的和平，确保了丝路东段的畅通。'昭君出塞'成为后世文学、绘画中反复演绎的经典主题。
figures.description_en: One of the Four Beauties of ancient China, she married the Xiongnu Chanyu Huhanye in a peace-making union. Her marriage secured over 50 years of peace between Han and Xiongnu, ensuring the safety of the Silk Road's eastern segment. Her story became an enduring theme in Chinese literature and art.

figures.name: 义净
figures.description: 与法显、玄奘并称三大求法僧。671年从广州乘波斯商船出发，经室利佛逝（今苏门答腊）抵达印度那烂陀寺学习十载，带回佛经四百余部。他记录了海上丝绸之路的详细航路，是研究7世纪南海贸易的珍贵史料。
figures.description_en: One of China's three great pilgrim monks alongside Faxian and Xuanzang. In 671 he departed Guangzhou on a Persian merchant ship, reached Nalanda Monastery in India via Srivijaya (Sumatra), studied for ten years, and returned with over 400 texts. His records detail the Maritime Silk Road's navigation and 7th-century South China Sea trade.

figures.name: 安禄山
figures.description: 粟特-突厥混血的唐代边将，其父为粟特商人、母为突厥巫师。他的身份本身就是丝路文化融合的缩影。755年发动安史之乱，唐朝由盛转衰，陆上丝路随之式微，客观上推动了海上丝路的崛起。
figures.description_en: A Tang frontier general of mixed Sogdian-Turkic descent — his father was a Sogdian merchant, his mother a Turkic shaman. His identity epitomizes Silk Road cultural fusion. His rebellion in 755 began the Tang dynasty's decline, and the overland Silk Road waned with it, accelerating the rise of the maritime route.

figures.name: 花剌子密
figures.description: 巴格达智慧宫的波斯学者，'算法'(Algorithm)一词即源自其名。他将印度的十进制计数法和中国的数学知识（通过丝路传入）融会贯通，著《代数学》。没有丝路的知识流动，就没有欧洲近代数学的起点。
figures.description_en: A Persian scholar at Baghdad's House of Wisdom — the word 'algorithm' derives from his name. He synthesized Indian decimal notation with Chinese mathematical knowledge (transmitted via the Silk Road) and wrote 'Algebra.' Without the Silk Road's flow of knowledge, there would have been no starting point for modern European mathematics.

figures.name: 郑和
figures.description: 明代伟大的航海家，原名马和。七次率领当时世界上最庞大的船队下西洋，航程遍及东南亚、印度、阿拉伯半岛和东非，比哥伦布早了近一个世纪。宝船长约137米，是当时世界上最大的木船。
figures.description_en: The great Ming dynasty navigator. He led seven epic voyages across the Indian Ocean, reaching Southeast Asia, India, Arabia, and East Africa — nearly a century before Columbus. His treasure ships were about 137 meters long, the largest wooden vessels of their time.

figures.name: 汪大渊
figures.description: 元代民间航海家，两次从泉州出发远航，足迹遍布东南亚、印度、阿拉伯和东非。著有《岛夷志略》，记录了220多个国家和地区的地理、物产和风土人情，是中国第一部系统的海外地理著作。
figures.description_en: A Yuan dynasty private navigator who sailed twice from Quanzhou, reaching Southeast Asia, India, Arabia, and East Africa. He wrote 'A Brief Account of Island Barbarians,' documenting over 220 countries and regions — China's first systematic work of overseas geography.

figures.name: 周达观
figures.description: 元代使节，随使团前往真腊（今柬埔寨），在吴哥居住一年。著有《真腊风土记》，详细记录了吴哥王朝的繁荣景象，是研究吴哥文明的唯一第一手文字史料。
figures.description_en: A Yuan dynasty envoy who traveled to Zhenla (modern Cambodia) and lived in Angkor for a year. His book 'The Customs of Cambodia' provides the only first-hand written account of the Angkor civilization at its height.

goods.name: 丝绸
goods.description: 丝路最核心的商品，也是这条路名称的由来。中国的养蚕缫丝技术长期被严格保密，丝绸在罗马帝国与黄金等价。
goods.description_en: The signature commodity that gave the Silk Road its name. China's sericulture techniques were a closely guarded secret for centuries, and silk was worth its weight in gold in the Roman Empire.

goods.name: 瓷器
goods.description: 中国瓷器通过海陆丝路远销世界，'China'一词即源于瓷器。伊斯兰世界和欧洲竞相仿制，但直到18世纪才破解制瓷秘密。
goods.description_en: Chinese porcelain was exported worldwide via both land and maritime Silk Roads — the very word 'china' derives from it. The Islamic world and Europe competed to imitate it but the secret was not cracked until the 18th century.

goods.name: 造纸术
goods.description: 公元751年怛罗斯之战后，被俘的中国造纸工匠将技术传入撒马尔罕，随后经巴格达传到欧洲。造纸术的传播深刻改变了世界文明进程。
goods.description_en: After the Battle of Talas (751 CE), captured Chinese papermakers brought the technology to Samarkand. From there it spread to Baghdad and eventually Europe, profoundly changing the course of world civilization.

goods.name: 玻璃器皿
goods.description: 罗马和波斯的玻璃器皿沿丝路传入中国，深受贵族喜爱。在中国多地汉墓中出土了罗马玻璃制品。
goods.description_en: Roman and Persian glassware traveled east along the Silk Road and was highly prized by Chinese nobility. Roman glass artifacts have been unearthed from Han dynasty tombs across China.

goods.name: 葡萄
goods.description: 张骞出使西域后将葡萄引入中原。葡萄和葡萄酒文化从此在中国扎根，唐诗中'葡萄美酒夜光杯'成为千古名句。
goods.description_en: Grapes were introduced to China after Zhang Qian's missions to the Western Regions. Grape and wine culture took root — Tang poetry immortalized 'grape wine in luminous cups' as an enduring image.

goods.name: 香料
goods.description: 胡椒、乳香、没药等香料通过海陆丝路进入中国，深刻影响了中国的饮食、医药和宗教仪式。
goods.description_en: Pepper, frankincense, myrrh, and other spices entered China via both land and maritime routes, profoundly influencing Chinese cuisine, medicine, and religious rituals.

goods.name: 茶叶
goods.description: 唐宋以后茶叶逐渐成为丝路大宗贸易商品。唐代陆羽著《茶经》，茶文化臻于成熟。通过茶马古道和丝路传入中亚、俄罗斯、阿拉伯和欧洲，最终改变了全世界的饮品习惯。
goods.description_en: After the Tang-Song period, tea became a major Silk Road trade commodity. Lu Yu's 'Classic of Tea' codified tea culture. Via the Tea Horse Road and the Silk Road, tea spread to Central Asia, Russia, the Arab world, and Europe — changing drinking habits worldwide.

goods.name: 西域良马
goods.description: 大宛（今乌兹别克斯坦费尔干纳盆地）出产的汗血宝马，是汉朝最渴望的军事物资。汉武帝两次派兵远征大宛以求良马，足见其对丝绸之路军事战略的重要性。
goods.description_en: The 'blood-sweating' heavenly horses of Ferghana were the most coveted military asset of the Han dynasty. Emperor Wu launched two military expeditions to Dayuan (Ferghana) to acquire them — testament to their strategic importance on the Silk Road.

goods.name: 佛教
goods.description: 佛教沿丝路从印度经中亚传入中国，是最具深远影响的丝路文化传播。犍陀罗艺术（希腊化佛教造像）经西域传入中原，深刻改变了中国的雕塑、绘画和思想世界。
goods.description_en: Buddhism traveled along the Silk Road from India through Central Asia to China — the most profound cultural transmission along the route. Gandharan art (Hellenistic Buddhist sculpture) entered China via the Western Regions, transforming Chinese sculpture, painting, and thought.

goods.name: 和田玉
goods.description: 和田出产的白玉早在丝路开通前数千年就传入中原，商代妇好墓中就出土了大量和田玉。玉石之路被认为是丝绸之路的前身，其路线后来被丝路沿用。
goods.description_en: White jade from Khotan reached the Chinese heartland thousands of years before the Silk Road was formalized — Shang dynasty tombs have yielded large quantities of Khotan jade. The 'Jade Road' is considered a precursor to the Silk Road, whose routes it followed.

goods.name: 火药
goods.description: 中国炼丹术士偶然发现的发明，13世纪通过蒙古西征传入伊斯兰世界和欧洲。火药终结了骑士时代和城堡时代，彻底改变了世界战争格局，是丝路末期最重大的技术输出之一。
goods.description_en: An accidental discovery by Chinese alchemists, gunpowder reached the Islamic world and Europe in the 13th century via the Mongol conquests. It ended the age of knights and castles, fundamentally reshaping warfare — one of the most consequential technology transfers of the Silk Road's later period.

goods.name: 指南针
goods.description: 中国发明的导航工具，12世纪经阿拉伯商人传入地中海世界。指南针使远洋航行成为可能，直接催生了欧洲的地理大发现时代，也推动了海上丝绸之路的繁荣。
goods.description_en: A Chinese navigational invention that reached the Mediterranean via Arab traders in the 12th century. The compass made ocean navigation possible, directly enabling Europe's Age of Discovery and driving the prosperity of the Maritime Silk Road.

goods.name: 苜蓿
goods.description: 张骞从西域带回的重要饲料作物，专门用来喂养大宛汗血宝马。苜蓿在中国广泛种植，支撑了汉唐骑兵的壮大，其重要性不亚于马匹本身——没有苜蓿就没有中国骑兵的黄金时代。
goods.description_en: A critical fodder crop brought back by Zhang Qian from the Western Regions, specifically to feed the prized Ferghana horses. Alfalfa was widely cultivated in China and sustained the Han and Tang cavalry — its importance rivaled that of the horses themselves. Without alfalfa, there would have been no golden age of Chinese cavalry.

goods.name: 粟特银器
goods.description: 粟特工匠制作的精美金银器，融合波斯、希腊和草原艺术风格。通过粟特商人传入唐朝，深受贵族喜爱，影响了唐代金银器的风格。粟特银器是丝路多元文化融合的典型物质见证。
goods.description_en: Exquisite gold and silver vessels crafted by Sogdian artisans, blending Persian, Greek, and steppe artistic traditions. Brought to Tang China by Sogdian merchants, they were prized by the nobility and profoundly influenced Tang metalwork. Sogdian silver is a tangible testament to the Silk Road's multicultural fusion.

goods.name: 乳香
goods.description: 从乳香树的树脂中提取的珍贵香料，用于宗教仪式、医药和化妆品。主要产于阿曼、也门和索马里，通过海上丝路大量输入中国。宋代香药贸易中乳香占据最大份额。
goods.description_en: A precious aromatic resin from the Boswellia tree, used in religious ceremonies, medicine, and cosmetics. Mainly produced in Oman, Yemen, and Somalia, it was imported in vast quantities to China via the Maritime Silk Road — frankincense dominated Song dynasty incense trade.

goods.name: 没药
goods.description: 与乳香齐名的珍贵树脂，用作防腐、镇痛和宗教仪式。唐代《新修本草》首次记载没药的药用价值，海上贸易使其成为中国药材中的重要一味。
goods.description_en: A precious resin alongside frankincense, used for preservation, pain relief, and religious rituals. First recorded in Chinese medicine during the Tang dynasty, maritime trade made it an important ingredient in traditional Chinese pharmacology.

goods.name: 象牙
goods.description: 非洲和印度的大象象牙通过海上丝路大量输入中国，用于制作精美的雕刻品、朝笏和装饰品。宋代广州市舶司的进口清单中象牙是最大宗商品之一。
goods.description_en: Elephant tusks from Africa and India were imported in large quantities via the Maritime Silk Road for exquisite carvings, court tablets, and ornaments. Ivory was one of the largest commodities in Guangzhou's customs records during the Song dynasty.

goods.name: 红珊瑚
goods.description: 地中海和红海出产的珍贵红珊瑚，是佛教七宝之一。通过海上丝路输入，用于制作佛珠、首饰和朝珠。被视为富贵和吉祥的象征。
goods.description_en: Precious red coral from the Mediterranean and Red Sea, counted among the Seven Treasures of Buddhism. Imported via the Maritime Silk Road for prayer beads, jewelry, and court ornaments — a symbol of wealth and good fortune.

goods.name: 丁香
goods.description: 原产于印度尼西亚马鲁古群岛的珍贵香料。汉代已有使用丁香的记载，用于口含除臭和调味。欧洲人对香料的渴望最终促成了大航海时代的开启。
goods.description_en: A precious spice native to Indonesia's Maluku Islands (the legendary Spice Islands). Already used in Han dynasty China as a breath freshener and seasoning. Europe's craving for cloves and other spices eventually launched the Age of Discovery.

events.name: 张骞出使西域
events.description: 汉武帝派遣张骞出使大月氏，欲联合抗击匈奴。张骞被匈奴扣押十年，逃脱后继续西行，最远抵达大夏（今阿富汗北部）。虽未达成军事同盟，但开启了汉朝与西域各国的交往。
events.description_en: Emperor Wu of Han dispatched Zhang Qian to seek an alliance with the Yuezhi against the Xiongnu. Held captive for ten years, Zhang Qian escaped and continued west, reaching as far as Bactria. Though no military alliance was formed, this opened relations with the Western Regions.

events.name: 怛罗斯之战
events.description: 唐军与阿拉伯帝国（大食）军队在怛罗斯河畔交战。唐军战败，但被俘的造纸工匠将造纸术传入中亚，成为技术传播史上的标志性事件。
events.description_en: Tang and Abbasid (Arab) forces clashed on the banks of the Talas River. The Tang forces were defeated, but captured Chinese papermakers brought papermaking technology to Central Asia — a landmark event in the history of technology transfer.

events.name: 玄奘西行
events.description: 玄奘法师从长安出发，偷渡出境，经敦煌、高昌、中亚，抵达印度那烂陀寺。17年后携带657部梵文佛经回到长安，唐太宗亲自接见。
events.description_en: Monk Xuanzang departed Chang'an, traveling via Dunhuang, Gaochang, and Central Asia to reach Nalanda Monastery in India. He returned 17 years later with 657 Sanskrit Buddhist texts and was personally received by Emperor Taizong.

events.name: 蒙古和平时期
events.description: 蒙古帝国统一欧亚大陆大部分地区后，丝路进入最安全和繁荣的时期。商队、使节、传教士（如马可·波罗）得以自由往来，东西方交流空前活跃。
events.description_en: After the Mongol Empire unified much of Eurasia, the Silk Road entered its safest and most prosperous period. Merchants, envoys, and missionaries (like Marco Polo) could travel freely — East-West exchange flourished as never before.

events.name: 郑和下西洋
events.description: 明代航海家郑和七次远航，到达东南亚、印度、阿拉伯和东非。这标志着海上丝绸之路的巅峰，也展现了中国与世界交流的另一条大动脉。
events.description_en: The Ming admiral Zheng He led seven grand voyages reaching Southeast Asia, India, Arabia, and East Africa. This marked the zenith of the Maritime Silk Road, showcasing another artery of China's exchange with the world.

events.name: 班超定西域
events.name_en: Ban Chao Pacifies the Western Regions
events.description: 班超率三十六人出使西域，'不入虎穴焉得虎子'——夜袭匈奴使者，威震鄯善。随后三十年间陆续平定疏勒、莎车、龟兹等国，使西域五十余国归附东汉，丝绸之路南道重开。
events.description_en: Ban Chao set out for the Western Regions with only 36 men. His legendary night raid on Xiongnu envoys — 'If you don't enter the tiger's den, how will you get the cubs?' — awed the kingdoms. Over 30 years he brought more than 50 states under Han control, reopening the southern Silk Road.

events.name: 法显西行
events.description: 六十岁的法显从长安出发，经陆路至印度求法，又取海路经斯里兰卡、苏门答腊回国，历时十五年。他是中国第一位完成陆去海还完整环线的求法僧，其《佛国记》为后世玄奘提供了重要指引。
events.description_en: At age 60, Faxian departed Chang'an, reached India overland in search of Buddhist scriptures, and returned by sea via Sri Lanka and Sumatra — a 15-year journey. He was the first Chinese monk to complete the full overland-there, maritime-back circuit, and his 'Record of Buddhist Kingdoms' guided Xuanzang a century later.

events.name: 大秦景教碑
events.description: 大秦景教流行中国碑在长安树立，记载了基督教聂斯脱里派（景教）从波斯经丝路传入中国的历史。碑文以汉文和叙利亚文双语书写，是东西方宗教交流的重要物证。
events.description_en: The Nestorian Stele was erected in Chang'an, recording the history of Nestorian Christianity (Jingjiao) entering China from Persia via the Silk Road. Inscribed in both Chinese and Syriac, it is a vital artifact of East-West religious exchange.

events.name: 安史之乱
events.description: 粟特裔将领安禄山发动叛乱，唐朝由此由盛转衰。战后吐蕃占领河西走廊，唐朝失去对西域的控制，陆上丝绸之路逐渐衰落，海上丝绸之路取而代之。这一事件永久改变了丝路格局。
events.description_en: The Sogdian-descended general An Lushan rebelled, marking the Tang dynasty's turning point from zenith to decline. After the war, Tibet occupied the Hexi Corridor, Tang lost control of the Western Regions, and the overland Silk Road gradually declined — replaced by the Maritime Silk Road. This event permanently reshaped the Silk Road.

events.name: 昭君出塞
events.name_en: Wang Zhaojun's Marriage to the Xiongnu
events.description: 宫女王昭君自请出嫁匈奴呼韩邪单于，汉元帝以公主之礼送嫁。和亲后汉匈边境逾五十年无大战事，商旅得以安全穿行于长城内外，为丝绸之路东段的长期稳定创造了条件。
events.description_en: Palace lady Wang Zhaojun volunteered to marry the Xiongnu Chanyu Huhanye, and Emperor Yuan of Han sent her off with full princess honors. The marriage secured over 50 years of peace on the Han-Xiongnu border, allowing caravans to safely cross the Great Wall region and ensuring long-term stability on the Silk Road's eastern segment.

events.name: 义净南海求法
events.description: 义净从广州搭乘波斯商船出海，经室利佛逝至印度，历时二十四年。他撰写的《南海寄归内法传》和《大唐西域求法高僧传》记录了海上丝路沿线的政治、贸易和宗教状况，是研究7世纪印度洋-南海贸易圈的珍贵文献。
events.description_en: Yijing departed Guangzhou on a Persian merchant ship, traveling via Srivijaya to India in a journey spanning 24 years. His two surviving works document the politics, trade, and religion along the Maritime Silk Road, providing invaluable records of the 7th-century Indian Ocean-South China Sea trading network.

events.name: 粟特商队网络形成
events.name_en: Rise of the Sogdian Trade Network
events.description: 粟特人是丝路上最卓越的商业民族，建立了从撒马尔罕到长安的庞大贸易网络。他们在丝路沿线设立殖民据点（如敦煌、凉州），控制着丝绸、马匹、珍宝的流通。粟特语一度成为丝路的国际通用商业语言。
events.description_en: The Sogdians were the Silk Road's greatest merchant people, building a vast trade network from Samarkand to Chang'an. They established colonial settlements along the route (Dunhuang, Liangzhou) and controlled the flow of silk, horses, and luxury goods. The Sogdian language became the Silk Road's international commercial lingua franca.

events.name: 市舶司设立
events.description: 唐玄宗在广州设立中国历史上第一个市舶司，专门管理海上贸易、征收关税和接待外国使节。此后宋元明各朝在广州、泉州、明州（宁波）、杭州等地广设市舶司，海上丝路进入官方管理时代。
events.description_en: Emperor Xuanzong of Tang established China's first maritime trade office in Guangzhou (714 CE) to regulate sea trade, collect customs, and receive foreign envoys. Subsequent dynasties set up similar offices in Quanzhou, Ningbo, and Hangzhou — the Maritime Silk Road entered an era of official administration.

events.name: 广州通海夷道
events.name_en: Guangzhou Maritime Route to Foreign Lands
events.description: 唐代地理学家贾耽在《皇华四达记》中详细记录的海上航线：从广州出发，经南海、马六甲海峡、印度洋至波斯湾和东非。这是人类历史上最早有精确记录的远洋航线之一，全程约14000公里。
events.description_en: The maritime route meticulously recorded by Tang geographer Jia Dan: departing Guangzhou, crossing the South China Sea, through the Malacca Strait, across the Indian Ocean to the Persian Gulf and East Africa. One of the earliest precisely documented transoceanic routes in human history — approximately 14,000 km.

routes.name: 丝绸西传之路
routes.description: 丝绸从长安出发，穿越河西走廊、塔里木盆地，翻越帕米尔高原，经波斯到达罗马帝国。罗马贵族为之疯狂，凯撒曾披丝绸出现在剧场引起轰动。
routes.description_en: Silk traveled from Chang'an through the Hexi Corridor, across the Tarim Basin and Pamirs, through Persia to Rome. Roman elites were obsessed — Caesar caused a sensation wearing silk at the theatre.

segments.relation: 河西走廊 → 丝路咽喉
塔里木盆地 → 翻越葱岭
中亚枢纽 → 粟特商人接力
segments.relation_en: Central Asian hub → Sogdian merchants relay

segments.relation: 波斯帝国 → 阿拔斯王朝
两河流域 → 拜占庭帝国

routes.name: 造纸术西传之路
routes.description: 公元751年怛罗斯之战，唐军造纸工匠被俘至撒马尔罕，造纸术由此传入阿拉伯世界，经巴格达、大马士革，最终在12世纪抵达欧洲，改变了人类文明的进程。
routes.description_en: After the 751 CE Battle of Talas, captured Tang papermakers brought the craft to Samarkand. From there papermaking spread through Baghdad and Damascus, reaching Europe by the 12th century — transforming civilization.

segments.relation: 怛罗斯之战 → 造纸工匠被俘西传
segments.relation_en: Battle of Talas → captured artisans spread the craft

segments.relation: 阿拉伯帝国 → 智慧宫抄写热潮
segments.relation_en: Arab Empire → House of Wisdom transcription boom

segments.relation: 经大马士革 → 12世纪抵欧洲

routes.name: 佛教东传之路
routes.description: 佛教从印度经犍陀罗传入西域，沿丝路东渐。鸠摩罗什在长安译经，玄奘西行取经，法显写下《佛国记》。莫高窟壁画是这场千年传播的结晶。
routes.description_en: Buddhism traveled from India through Gandhara into the Western Regions, then eastward along the Silk Road. Kumarajiva translated sutras in Chang'an, Xuanzang journeyed west for scriptures, Faxian recorded his travels — the Mogao Caves are the crystallization of this millennium-long transmission.

segments.relation: 犍陀罗 → 西域佛国于阗
塔里木南缘 → 丝路交汇
segments.relation_en: Southern Tarim rim → Silk Road junction

segments.relation: 穿越塔克拉玛干 → 莫高窟
河西走廊 → 东汉都城
中原弘法 → 译经中心

routes.name: 指南针航海之路
routes.description: 指南针从中国泉州出发，随郑和宝船下西洋，经马六甲、印度洋抵达阿拉伯和东非。阿拉伯商人又将航海罗盘传入地中海，为大航海时代提供了关键技术。
routes.description_en: The compass sailed from Quanzhou with Zheng He's treasure fleet through Malacca and across the Indian Ocean to Arabia and East Africa. Arab merchants then carried the navigational compass to the Mediterranean, providing key technology for the Age of Discovery.

segments.relation: 东南沿海 → 南海门户
segments.relation_en: Southeast coast → South China Sea gateway

segments.relation: 南海 → 马六甲海峡
印度洋东段 → 香料海岸
阿拉伯海 → 波斯湾门户
波斯湾 → 红海入口
红海 → 地中海终点

routes.name: 香料东传之路
routes.description: 乳香、没药从阿拉伯半岛和东非出发，经红海、印度洋抵达广州。宋代香药贸易中乳香占最大份额，中国宫廷和寺庙中香烟缭绕数千年。
routes.description_en: Frankincense and myrrh traveled from Arabia and East Africa through the Red Sea and Indian Ocean to Guangzhou. Frankincense dominated Song dynasty incense trade — Chinese palaces and temples were filled with their fragrance for millennia.

segments.relation: 红海 → 波斯湾集散
阿拉伯海 → 印度香料海岸
印度洋 → 马六甲转运
南海 → 广州市舶司
内河水陆联运 → 帝都长安

routes.name: 瓷器海上丝路
routes.description: 中国瓷器从泉州出发，经南海、印度洋抵达阿拉伯和东非。阿拉伯人称之为'中国雪'(al-Ṣīnī)，欧洲人称之为'白色黄金'。至今在东非海滩上仍能发现宋代瓷片。
routes.description_en: Chinese porcelain sailed from Quanzhou through the South China Sea and Indian Ocean to Arabia and East Africa. Arabs called it 'al-Ṣīnī' (Chinese ware), Europeans called it 'white gold.' Song dynasty shards are still found on East African beaches today.

segments.relation: 东南大港 → 南海门户
segments.relation_en: Southeast great port → South China Sea gateway

segments.relation: 南海 → 马六甲海峡
印度洋东段 → 锡兰宝石岛
segments.relation_en: Eastern Indian Ocean → Isle of Gems

segments.relation: 印度洋 → 波斯湾入口
波斯湾 → 红海阿拉伯世界
segments.relation_en: Persian Gulf → Red Sea / Arab world

segments.relation: 红海 → 东非最远到达点
segments.relation_en: Red Sea → farthest point in East Africa

routes.name: 火药西传之路
routes.description: 火药发明于唐代，13世纪蒙古西征时将火药技术传入阿拉伯和欧洲。欧洲人将火药用于火器，反过来改变了战争形态和世界格局。从炼丹炉里的偶然发现到改变世界的技术，火药走过了最传奇的西传之路。
routes.description_en: Invented in Tang China, gunpowder spread to Arabia and Europe during the 13th-century Mongol conquests. Europeans adapted it for firearms, transforming warfare and the global order. From an alchemist's accidental discovery to a world-changing technology — gunpowder's journey west is legendary.

segments.relation: 唐代炼丹术 → 火药配方成熟
蒙古西征 → 火药武器传入中亚
segments.relation_en: Mongol conquest → gunpowder weapons reach Central Asia

segments.relation: 经波斯 → 阿拉伯世界掌握火药
segments.relation_en: Via Persia → Arab world masters gunpowder

segments.relation: 传入欧洲 → 火器改变战争形态

routes.name: 茶马古道
routes.description: 茶马古道是连接中国西南与西藏、南亚的古老商道。云南的茶叶由马帮驮运，翻越横断山脉进入西藏，换取藏区的良马。这条路比丝绸之路更险峻——悬崖峭壁上的马帮铃声，在大山中回响了千年。
routes.description_en: The Tea Horse Road was an ancient trade route connecting southwest China with Tibet and South Asia. Yunnan tea was carried by horse caravans across the Hengduan Mountains into Tibet, traded for fine Tibetan horses. More treacherous than the Silk Road — the sound of caravan bells echoed through these mountains for a thousand years.

segments.relation: 中原茶文化 → 东汉都城
segments.relation_en: Central Plains tea culture → Eastern Han capital

segments.relation: 河西走廊 → 丝路咽喉
塔里木南缘 → 西域佛国于阗
翻越喀喇昆仑 → 印度次大陆
